genValidProfiles.name: pass-oi-default-scalar
genValidProfiles.intent: Seed-indexed OI-D scalar trigger-smoke modules covering direct scalar identities plus local-carried and local.tee/commuted add-zero variants; future work should replace the deterministic three-case selector with broader randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and existing-producer wrappers.
genValidProfiles.ordinaryCommand: bun scripts/pass-fuzz-compare.ts --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --pass optimize-instructions --gen-valid-profile pass-oi-default-scalar --out-dir .tmp/pass-fuzz-oi-default-scalar --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe
genValidProfiles.implementation: src/validate/gen_valid.mbt special-cases this profile to emit one of three validating trigger-smoke modules selected by seed modulo 3: direct scalar, local-carried scalar, or local.tee/commuted scalar. src/validate/gen_valid_tests.mbt asserts both baseline opcode coverage and distinct seed-indexed trigger signatures.

genValidProfiles.name: pass-oi-memory-bulk
genValidProfiles.intent: Seed-indexed and BodyStream-randomized OI-G trigger-smoke-plus modules covering direct i32 tiny memory.copy/fill lowering plus offset folding, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk operands, live private-global plus dead-trap boundary shells, no-param call/existing-producer-wrapped tiny bulk operations, narrow-store mask boundaries, dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands, multi-memory bulk operations, seed-derived nonzero in-page copy/read addresses, seed-derived non-primary-memory in-page copy/read addresses, seed-derived private-destination cross-memory random-address copy/read addresses, seed-derived memory64 private-destination cross-memory random-address copy/read addresses, seed-derived source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, seed-derived memory64 source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, a Node-runtime-compatible single-memory restore/copy/read exported function with active data, a source-visible shared-memory wait/notify/load profile case, a Node-runtime-compatible single-memory fill/restore/read exported function with active data, a memory64 fill/restore/read case with active data, a multi-memory fill/restore/read case with active destination data, and a memory64+multi-memory fill/restore/read case with active destination data on memory 1; still needs live randomized wrappers around existing memory producers, randomized atomic RMW/cmpxchg addresses, full-width/source-visible xchg beyond byte-width restore smoke, broader wait/notify beyond private nonblocking smoke, true shared-memory ordering, true live ordering, and broader randomized memory64/multi-memory coverage before signoff.
genValidProfiles.ordinaryCommand: bun scripts/pass-fuzz-compare.ts --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --pass optimize-instructions --gen-valid-profile pass-oi-memory-bulk --out-dir .tmp/pass-fuzz-oi-memory-bulk --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe
genValidProfiles.implementation: src/validate/gen_valid.mbt special-cases this profile to emit validating trigger-smoke modules selected from nineteen OI-G cases: direct tiny i32 bulk/load-store, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk, live effect plus dead trap boundary, existing-producer/no-param-call wrapped bulk, narrow store-mask boundaries, dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands, multi-memory bulk operations, seed-derived nonzero in-page copy/read addresses, seed-derived non-primary-memory copy/read addresses, seed-derived private-destination cross-memory copy/read addresses, seed-derived memory64 private-destination cross-memory copy/read addresses, seed-derived source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, seed-derived memory64 source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, a Node-runtime-compatible single-memory restore/copy/read exported function with active data, a source-visible shared-memory wait/notify/load exported function, a runtime-compatible single-memory fill/restore/read exported function with active data, a memory64 fill/restore/read function with active data, a multi-memory fill/restore/read function with active destination data, or a memory64+multi-memory fill/restore/read function with active destination data on memory 1. src/validate/gen_valid_tests.mbt asserts stable labels, validation, memory64 coverage, multi-memory/cross-memory coverage, source-visible restore coverage, memory64 restore coverage, runtime-compatible export/restore/fill coverage, memory64 fill/restore coverage, multi-memory fill/restore coverage, and distinct trigger signatures.

rows.notes: pass-oi-default-scalar now emits three deterministic seed-indexed trigger-smoke cases for direct, local-carried, and local.tee/commuted OI-D shapes instead of one fixed module. The count-3 grouped OI-D sweep reaches comparison and reports three raw mismatches with no validation/generator/property/command failures; classify them as open scalar parity evidence until implemented or measured, not automatic semantic-safe drift. Future work should replace the seed-indexed smoke selector with randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and wrappers around existing producers. The sweep runner prints result summaries (and can load them with --summarize-existing), so future OI-D/OI-E triage should start from profile-case and case-label status groups before opening individual WAT diffs. A 2026-06-27 representative diff pass classified the three trigger labels in classifiedCaseLabels. The narrow literal double-eqz final-folding candidate is now implemented and covered. The fresh count-3 OI-D sweep shows residual mismatches only from Starshine extra folds of constant compare and/or constant sign-extension shapes; those sampled residuals are measured smaller Starshine outputs (-5/-2/-2 canonical wasm bytes) and all outputs validate, but the row remains open until broader randomized trigger coverage and/or source-backed family policy decides whether to retain or align these output-shape wins.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-default-scalar:local-tee-commuted-add-zero
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant compare and constant sign-extension folds
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps one pure constant i32.eq and explicit constant sign-extension opcodes; Starshine folds both constant i32.eq forms and direct constant sign extensions to constants. After the literal double-eqz fix both sides agree on i32.const 1. The sampled Starshine canonical wasm is 91 bytes vs Binaryen 96 bytes (-5 bytes), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 fresh OI-D count-3 sweep at .tmp/oi-parity-sweep-oi-d-double-eqz-fix-smoke; stat measured canonical wasm byte deltas; wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine canonical outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-default-scalar:direct
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant sign-extension folds
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps direct constant sign-extension opcodes; Starshine folds those direct constant sign extensions to constants. After the literal double-eqz fix both sides agree on i32.const 1. The sampled Starshine canonical wasm is 63 bytes vs Binaryen 65 bytes (-2 bytes), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 fresh OI-D count-3 sweep at .tmp/oi-parity-sweep-oi-d-double-eqz-fix-smoke; stat measured canonical wasm byte deltas; wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine canonical outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-default-scalar:local-carried-add-zero
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant sign-extension folds
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen and Starshine both fold local-carried add-zero and the literal double-eqz representative to constants. Residual drift is Binaryen keeping direct constant sign-extension opcodes while Starshine folds them to constants. The sampled Starshine canonical wasm is 71 bytes vs Binaryen 73 bytes (-2 bytes), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.

rows.notes: pass-oi-local-facts now emits three deterministic seed-indexed trigger-smoke cases for direct sign-extension facts, local.tee add-zero facts, and local-carried mask facts instead of one fixed module. The count-3 profile compare and grouped OI-E execute sweep both reach comparison and report three raw mismatches with no validation/generator/property/command failures; classify them as open LocalScanner parity gaps until inspected/implemented, not semantic-safe drift. Future work should replace the seed-indexed smoke selector with randomized maxBits/signExt/nullness fact constructors and existing-producer wrappers. The sweep runner now prints result summaries (and can load them with --summarize-existing), so future OI-D/OI-E triage should start from profile-case and case-label status groups before opening individual WAT diffs. A 2026-06-27 representative diff pass classified the three trigger labels in classifiedCaseLabels. The local-fact redundant sign-extension removals look like smaller Starshine output, but remain only Starshine-win candidates until measured or otherwise source-backed.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-local-facts:local-carried-mask

classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps i32.extend8_s(local.get) and i64.extend32_s(local.get) after preceding constant local.set facts; Starshine removes both redundant sign extensions and leaves the later mask unchanged.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Measure size/perf or add source-backed semantic proof for local-fact redundant sign-extension removal before accepting as intentional Starshine drift; otherwise consider narrowing the trigger or aligning to Binaryen.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-local-facts:direct-sign-extension

classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Promote to an accepted Starshine win only with measured size/perf/semantic evidence; otherwise keep as open classified mismatch and avoid treating the raw mismatch as safe by default.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 diff of binaryen.wat vs starshine.wat in the representative failure dir from .tmp/oi-parity-sweep-summary-smoke.

classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Separate OI-D scalar constant-compare drift from OI-E local-fact sign-extension cleanup, then either measure and accept the smaller Starshine shapes or align the specific trigger expectations to Binaryen.

rows.blockerBoundaryReason: Starshine has many direct boolean/select probes, and the grouped OI-F transform sweep now has three classified mismatch labels plus one normalized match for the sampled private-global effectful-sibling representative. Pure synthetic if/select, dead-trap, and commuted-constant representatives remain measured Starshine-win candidates under their transform contracts. The private-global effectful-sibling representative is no longer a canonical size-losing sample after follow-up raw-skip scalar folding, signed-to-unsigned spelling cleanup for nonnegative constant compare/div/rem/shift forms, finite float sub-to-add-negative rewrites, and the sampled f64 div-by-two/sub spelling rewrite: Binaryen and Starshine normalized wasm are both 2893 bytes and the sampled float opcode counts now match. Raw Starshine remains larger (4135 vs Binaryen 2893), so raw encoder/structure size remains a separate classified residual rather than OI-F closure. Branch hints/no-fold/no-reorder metadata remain uncovered.

All measured raw/normalized outputs validate with wasm-tools validate --features all (2026-06-27).
rows.notes: oi-if-select-shell, oi-effectful-sibling, oi-trapping-sibling, and oi-commuted-operands are implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner. The first grouped count-4 OI-F sweep classifies all four transform labels before one-off probes: pure if/select, dead trapping sibling, and commuted-constant representatives are sampled canonical-size Starshine-win candidates under their transform contracts. Follow-up red-first slices reduced oi-effectful-sibling by sinking same-global if arms to global.set(if(result ...)) in HOT and raw-skip paths, folding sampled dropped/nested integer constants, rewriting sampled raw-skipped nonnegative signed compare/div/rem/shift spellings to unsigned forms, and matching the sampled finite float sub/div spellings. The fix7 grouped sweep now has transform:oi-effectful-sibling as a normalized match, but OI-F stays open because the other three transform labels still mismatch and raw Starshine bytes for the effectful sample remain larger despite identical normalized size and opcode counts.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-if-select-shell
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: Measured sampled Starshine-win candidate for pure synthetic constant if/select shells, mixed with broad pass-oi-boolean-select scalar output-shape drift; not OI-F family signoff.
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps two constant-condition if-result shells in the representative normalized WAT, while Starshine folds them to dropped constants and keeps a constant-select shell spelling difference. The transform inserts stack-neutral pure constant if/select shells, so these folded shells are side-effect-free for this sample. Fix7 measurement still validates both tools' raw and canonical outputs. Canonical wasm remains a Starshine win at 2732 bytes vs Binaryen 2844 bytes (-112); raw wasm remains Starshine-larger at 3841 vs Binaryen 2844, so the raw encoder/local-section size gap remains separate. Counts reflect the residual pure shell/scalar drift: Binaryen has if=4 and select=0 while Starshine has if=0 and select=2, with Starshine also emitting fewer i32/i64 constants in normalized WAT.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a sampled candidate only under the pure synthetic transform contract. Before accepting more broadly, add trigger-biased existing-producer wrappers and branch-hint/no-fold metadata coverage; if canonical wins disappear or live effects/traps enter the shell, reopen as a parity gap.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 OI-F fix7 grouped sweep at .tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix7-20260627; --summarize-existing reported this label still mismatches, measured raw/canonical wasm sizes and opcode counts, and wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine raw/canonical outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-effectful-sibling
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: Sampled private-global effectful-sibling representative now normalized-matches Binaryen after red-first raw-skip float spelling cleanup; this closes the sampled canonical size-losing/floating-spelling residual for this transform label, but not broader OI-F family closure or the raw-byte size gap.
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The grouped fix7 sweep reports transform:oi-effectful-sibling as a normalized match. Manual measurement of the same case-000002 input shows Binaryen and Starshine normalized wasm are both 2893 bytes, while raw Starshine remains larger at 4135 vs Binaryen 2893. Opcode counts now match for the previously residual sampled families: global.set 10/10; i32/i64 signed lt/gt/le/ge/div/rem/shr counts 0/0 and unsigned counts 4/4 per opcode family; f32.sub/add/div/mul 0/4/2/2 vs 0/4/2/2; f64.sub/add/div/mul 0/6/2/4 vs 0/6/2/4. Both tools' raw and normalized outputs validate with wasm-tools validate --features all. The raw-byte loss is now classified as residual raw encoder/structure/local-section drift on an otherwise normalized-matching sample, not as a remaining OI-D/OI-F float spelling mismatch.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Do not generalize this sampled normalized match to OI-F closure. Keep raw-size loss visible as a separate encoder/structure follow-up if raw bytes become in-scope, and continue grouped work on the remaining OI-F mismatching labels: oi-if-select-shell, oi-trapping-sibling, and oi-commuted-operands. Keep live trap/effect ordering and branch-hint/no-fold/no-reorder metadata separate.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 follow-up at .tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix7-20260627 after red-first raw-skip finite float sub and f64 div-by-two/sub tests. Focused new tests passed, optimize_instructions_test.mbt passed 534/534, moon test src/passes passed 3575/3575, native build passed with existing warnings, grouped OI-F sweep compared 4/4 with transform:oi-effectful-sibling as the sole normalized match, and wasm-tools validate --features all passed for manually measured Binaryen and Starshine raw/normalized effectful-sibling outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-trapping-sibling
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: Measured sampled Starshine-win candidate for a dead synthetic trap shell plus broad scalar cleanup; not live trap-order coverage.
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform inserts scalar div/rem-by-zero trap-shaped siblings under a dead i32.const 0 if. Starshine removes/folds more of the dead shell and constant-control debris than Binaryen in the representative. Fix7 measurement still validates both tools' raw and canonical outputs. Canonical wasm remains a Starshine win at 2705 bytes vs Binaryen 2845 (-140); raw wasm remains Starshine-larger at 3842 vs Binaryen 2845. Starshine emits fewer dropped roots and constants (drop 318 vs Binaryen 324, i32.const 144 vs 154, i64.const 108 vs 136), consistent with dead synthetic trap/control cleanup rather than a live trap-order proof.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as sampled only for dead synthetic trap siblings. Reopen as OI-F/OI-G/OI-J work for live-but-preserved trap ordering, memory/ref/GC trap lanes, or any validation/runtime evidence that dead-shell removal changes behavior.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 OI-F fix7 grouped sweep at .tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix7-20260627; --summarize-existing reported this label still mismatches, measured raw/canonical wasm sizes and opcode counts, and wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine raw/canonical outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-commuted-operands
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: Measured sampled Starshine-win candidate dominated by pure constant operand-order/scalar cleanup, with OI-D overlap; not a boolean/select shell closure.
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The representative has no remaining if/select/global.set/i32.add count difference after optimization, but normalized WAT still differs on broad scalar spelling from the pass-oi-boolean-select base profile plus the pure commuted-constant transform. Fix7 measurement still validates both tools' raw and canonical outputs. Canonical wasm remains a Starshine win at 2755 bytes vs Binaryen 2855 (-100); raw wasm remains Starshine-larger at 3882 vs Binaryen 2855. Counts show the residual is scalar cleanup rather than OI-F control shape: i32.and counts match at 6/6, while Binaryen keeps more i32.eq and i64.const spellings (i32.eq 6 vs Starshine 2, i64.const 132 vs Starshine 108).
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Route recurring scalar spelling to OI-D classification and keep OI-F focused on boolean/select/no-reorder triggers. Reopen if randomized commuted operands around live boolean/select producers produce size-losing or semantic drift.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 OI-F fix7 grouped sweep at .tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix7-20260627; --summarize-existing reported this label still mismatches, measured raw/canonical wasm sizes and opcode counts, and wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine raw/canonical outputs.

rows.title: Memory/load/store and bulk-memory tiny-lowering boundaries
rows.binaryenOracleMode: compare-pass normalized WAT with all-features; focused rows must keep trap-relaxed zero-size mode separate
rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The OI-G trigger profile has sixteen seed-indexed/BodyStream-randomized trigger-smoke-plus cases: direct tiny i32 bulk/load-store, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk, live private-global/dead-trap, existing-producer/no-param-call wrapped bulk, narrow-store masks, dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands, multi-memory bulk operations, seed-derived random-address copy/read, seed-derived non-primary-memory random-address copy/read, seed-derived private-destination cross-memory random-address copy/read, seed-derived memory64 private-destination cross-memory random-address copy/read, seed-derived source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, seed-derived memory64 source-visible cross-memory copy/read with destination-byte restore, a Node-runtime-compatible single-memory restore/copy/read exported function with active data, source-visible shared-memory wait/notify/load exported function coverage, and runtime-compatible single-memory fill/restore/read exported function coverage. The metamorphic layer now includes live zero/nonzero copy/fill/read, non-primary-memory self-copy/read, private-destination fixed-address cross-memory copy/read for memory32 and memory64 source/destination memories, varied/dynamic/end-byte copy/read, private-memory commuted constant address/store probes, and fixed/dynamic no-op atomic load smoke, non-primary/private-memory varied atomic-load smoke, and non-primary/private-memory no-op atomic store/RMW/cmpxchg smoke with byte-width xchg restore probes plus private shared-memory nonblocking wait/notify smoke. Prior canonical and raw size-losing grouped residuals have been reduced by red-first raw fallback/encoder fixes; the latest count400 memory-commuted grouped run fixes the newly exposed size-losing commuted-address raw-partial residual and still has store-mask low-byte-mask plus commuted/synthetic cleanup residuals, with zero raw-size-larger failure dirs in the 50 sampled failure dirs by raw wasm/normalized-WAT size review. OI-G remains open for grouped mismatch reduction, randomized atomic RMW/cmpxchg addresses beyond current module-derived smoke, full-width/source-visible xchg beyond byte-width restore smoke, broader wait/notify beyond private nonblocking smoke, true shared-memory ordering, broader source-visible mutation/restore shapes, true live memory effect/trap ordering, broader store/address commutation beyond the new private-memory commuted address/store transform, randomized memory64/multi-memory wrappers, raw-size residual policy, exact cross-memory runtime execution evidence where needed, and broader generator evidence. OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT remains blocked until true descriptor-compatible evidence exists.

rows.notes: The latest grouped OI-G run is .tmp/oi-g-memory64-multimemory-fill-restore-count513-20260629 after adding the nineteenth pass-oi-memory-bulk trigger-smoke-plus case, oi-memory-bulk:memory64-multi-memory-fill-restore-read. It compared 513/513 cases with 456 normalized matches, 57 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, and Binaryen cache hits/misses 269/244. The new profile case is a two-memory memory64 active-data fill/restore/read function: save a destination byte from memory 1 with i64.load8_u, perform length-1 memory.fill on memory 1 with i64 address/size operands, read the filled byte from memory 1, restore the saved byte with i64.store8 to memory 1, and return the filled value. Direct count100 sampled all nineteen labels with 95 normalized matches, five known store-mask residuals, zero failures, and memory64-multi-memory-fill-restore-read 6/6 matches. Grouped count513 samples memory64-multi-memory-fill-restore-read 24 times with 23 matches and one mismatch; the residual is under oi-commuted-operands and differs only by Starshine folding a dropped pure reverse i32.eq(42, 7) to i32.const 0 while Binaryen keeps the compare. rows.notes: oi-call-ref-target-wrapper is implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner: it appends a private no-op target and prepends direct ref.func/call_ref, block-returned ref.func/call_ref, typed-select target/call_ref, and dead call_ref shells to existing defined functions. A default GenValid one-case compare produced one raw mismatch with no validation/generator/command failures; the earlier pass-oi-call-ref seed-0x5eed validity blocker from stringref/exn ref pools is fixed and the profile-backed smoke now reaches comparison. The profile-backed representative first exposed a large size-losing OI-H parity gap where Binaryen directized synthetic ref.func call_ref wrappers while Starshine left call_ref/ref.func spelling. The raw skip path now directizes adjacent ref.func/block-ref.func, constant-select ref.func, and single-use local.set(ref.func)/local.get call_ref/return_call_ref wrappers, and it trims guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast trap tails before returning a skipped function, without widening the full HOT raw gate. The latest one-case target-wrapper smoke still mismatches, but OI-H call target spelling is no longer the sampled residual: Starshine and Binaryen both have 0 call_ref/table.get residuals, 20 direct `call $5` occurrences, 0 ref.test/ref.cast/br_on_cast/br_on_cast_fail, and five `unreachable` roots. The remaining 18-byte raw size gap is local-section/ref.func-drop output shape drift: Starshine drops the inserted `ref.func` uses while Binaryen keeps five dropped `ref.func` instructions. A follow-up count-3 grouped OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` mixed the target-wrapper, local-carried, and effectful-sibling transforms; all three cases still mismatched, but every sampled Starshine and Binaryen output had call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, and table.get=0. The target-wrapper case remains the local-section/ref.func-drop shape drift; the local-carried case is local-section/dropped-null shape drift after successful call_ref cleanup; the effectful-sibling case is cross-family effectful private-global if/scalar spelling drift after successful call_ref cleanup. Future OI-H work should run broader table/local/return_call_ref/multivalue sweeps to find true remaining call_ref directization gaps instead of over-mining these cross-family residuals.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-call-ref-target-wrapper
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: OI-H private ref.func target-wrapper directization is fixed for the grouped representative; remaining mismatch is local-section/dropped-ref.func output-shape drift rather than sampled call_ref target spelling
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Input had 44 call_ref, 4 return_call_ref, and 56 ref.func occurrences. Binaryen and Starshine outputs both have call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, table.get=0, direct call $5=20, ref.test=0, ref.cast=0, br_on_cast=0, br_on_cast_fail=0, and unreachable=5. Binaryen keeps five dropped ref.func instructions and raw/canonical size 554 bytes; Starshine drops those uses, has raw 572 bytes and canonical 514 bytes. Both raw outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep this residual classified separately from OI-H target directization. Only revisit dropped-ref.func/local-section shape if grouped sweeps show a material size/perf regression; otherwise prioritize true live table-state known targets, return_call_ref, argument localization, multivalue targets, and target-boundary sweeps.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-ref-gc:direct-null-test-cast-eq
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: Reduced OI-I-owned direct null/test/cast/equality output-shape drift; sampled Starshine cleanup is semantically safe for the generated direct-null sequence and measured smaller after canonicalization, while the oi-effectful-sibling variant also carries separate OI-F/OI-D scalar/if spelling drift.
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Without metamorphic transforms, the same direct-null/test/cast trigger still mismatches: Binaryen keeps dropped ref.is_null(ref.null none) shells, keeps dropped nullable ref.null shells for nullable ref.test/ref.cast of null, then reaches the non-null ref.cast(null) unreachable; Starshine folds the dropped null tests to constants, removes one side-effect-free nullable ref.null shell before the guaranteed trap tail, and reaches the same unreachable. Both raw and canonical outputs validate; canonical wasm is Starshine 40 bytes vs Binaryen 48 bytes, raw wasm is 48 bytes each. The earlier oi-effectful-sibling representative remains mixed because it additionally differs on private-global if sinking and constant i32.add spelling.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Do not add a size-losing Binaryen-shape rewrite for this sampled pure dropped-null sequence unless broader randomized OI-I evidence shows a semantic, validation, or downstream regression. Next scale pass-oi-ref-gc direct-null/test/cast into randomized existing-producer wrappers and separately classify the oi-effectful-sibling scalar/if drift under OI-F/OI-D.

rows.notes: OI-M has extensive one-off boundary research through many effect-count rows. The family workflow now provides forwarded selected-lane, effectful-sibling, dead trap-shaped sibling, and local-carried smoke transforms, but still needs trigger-biased tuple-profile constructors and existing-producer/effect/trap selected-lane wrappers to stop producing one boundary doc per lane/effect count. The first synthetic `oi-tuple-selected-lane` smoke mismatch is classified in `classifiedCaseLabels`: for pure constant multivalue block lane extraction, Starshine keeps nested block-expression localization while Binaryen scalarizes to more straight-line local sets; the sampled Starshine canonical/raw wasm is smaller and both outputs validate, so this is a sampled Starshine-win candidate, not a closure of the broader tuple-scratch reconstruction family.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: pass-oi-tuple:oi-tuple-selected-lane-smoke
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: sampled size-winning representation drift over pure synthetic multivalue selected-lane smoke; broader OI-M tuple-scratch localization remains open
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform prepends pure constant multivalue block selected-lane shells. Binaryen scalarizes the shell into many straight-line local.set/local.tee operations; Starshine keeps nested block-result expressions around the selected lane. Starshine canonical wasm is 4100 bytes vs Binaryen 4326 bytes (-226), raw wasm is 3975 vs 4229 bytes (-254), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Do not treat this smoke as OI-M signoff. Upgrade pass-oi-tuple / oi-tuple-selected-lane to wrap existing tuple producers with effectful/trapping siblings and selected-lane variants; preserve this sample as a Starshine-win candidate unless broader probes show semantic or size regressions.
